title: Quiz: Feature Engineering for Fraud Detection
description: Test your understanding of fraud detection feature engineering

questions.question: What is a velocity feature in fraud detection?

options.text: The rate of transactions or spending over time windows

questions.explanation: Velocity features measure the rate of transactions or spending over various time windows (1 hour, 24 hours, 7 days, etc.). Unusual bursts of activity often indicate fraud, such as card testing or rapid account draining.

questions.question: What does 'impossible travel' detection identify?

options.text: Two transactions too far apart geographically given the time between them

questions.explanation: Impossible travel detection flags when two transactions occur in locations that would require traveling faster than physically possible (e.g., > 500 km/h). This indicates the card is being used fraudulently in multiple locations.

questions.question: Why compare a transaction amount to the customer's historical average?

options.text: To detect unusual spending that deviates from the customer's pattern

questions.explanation: Comparing to historical averages helps detect anomalous transactions. A $5,000 transaction might be normal for one customer but highly suspicious for another who typically spends $50. The z-score captures this deviation.

questions.question: How should features be normalized for quantum circuit encoding?

options.text: To [0, π] or similar ranges suitable for rotation angles

questions.explanation: Quantum circuits use rotation gates (RY, RZ) that take angle parameters. Features must be normalized to appropriate ranges like [0, π] for single rotations or [0, 2π] for full rotations to effectively encode the information.

questions.question: Network features that analyze merchant-customer relationships can help detect fraud rings.
questions.explanation: True. Fraud rings often share merchants or operate through specific network patterns. Analyzing the graph of card-to-merchant relationships can reveal suspicious clusters and connections that indicate organized fraud.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'is_new_category' feature?

options.text: To track new merchant categories added to the system
To flag when a customer transacts in a merchant category they've never used before

questions.explanation: The 'is_new_category' feature flags when a transaction occurs in a merchant category the customer has never used. First-time purchases at unusual category types (like wire transfers for a customer who only uses grocery stores) can indicate fraud.

questions.question: Why is log transformation often applied to transaction amounts?

options.text: To compress the wide range and handle the skewed distribution of amounts

questions.explanation: Transaction amounts typically follow a right-skewed distribution with most transactions being small and a few very large. Log transformation compresses this range, making the distribution more normal and improving model performance.

questions.question: Which are effective behavioral features for fraud detection? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Amount z-score compared to customer history
Transaction hour frequency for this customer
Distance from customer's typical transaction locations

questions.explanation: Amount z-score, hour frequency, and distance from typical locations are all behavioral features that compare current transaction to historical patterns. Customer name length is not relevant to fraud patterns.
